latest.title: Change of name and TIDM
latest.content:
Today 10:49
RNS Number : 5289O
Cykel AI PLC
12 January 2026
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended). Upon the publication of this announcement via a Regulatory Information Service, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain.
12 January 2026
DeFi Development Corporation UK PLC (formerly Cykel AI PLC)
(the "
Company
")
Change of name and TIDM
DeFi Development Corporation UK PLC (LSE: DFDV), a Main Market listed company which offers an AI agent platform that enables businesses to automate tasks through the use of specialised digital workers for
sales, recruitment and research and has adopted
a Solana focused digital asset treasury strategy,
is pleased to announce that the Company has changed its name and will trade under its new name DeFi Development Corporation UK PLC with effect from 12 January 2026.
In addition, with effect from 13 January 2026, the Company's TIDM will be amended from "CYK" to "DFDV" on the Main Market of the London Stock Exchange.
The decision to change name to
DeFi Development Corporation UK PLC
reflects the Company's continued development of its Solana focused digital asset treasury strategy as previously announced on 28 August 2025. The "Cykel" trading name will continue to be used for the Company's AI agent activities.
In addition, the Company's website address will also change to www.defidevcorp.co.uk.
Shareholders should note that their shareholdings will be unaffected by the change of name. Existing share certificates should be retained as they will remain valid for all purposes and no new share certificates will be issued.
The Company's ISIN and SEDOL will remain unchanged. The Company's Legal Entity Identifier remains 213800QEO6L6JAS62H02.
The Directors of the Company accept responsibility for this announcement.
*ENDS*
For further information:
Company
Michael Chan
Director

latest.title: Further re warrants
latest.content:
4 Dec 2025 07:00
RNS Number : 1455K
Cykel AI PLC
04 December 2025
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended). Upon the publication of this announcement via a Regulatory Information Service, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain.
4 December 2025
Cykel AI PLC
("
Cykel AI
" or the "
Company
")
Further re Warrants
Cykel AI PLC (LSE:CYK) announces the following with respect to the warrants, full details of which are set out in the Company's announcement of 28 August 2025.
Warrants cancellation
The Company announces that it has agreed to the cancellation of 5,000,000 Pre-Paid and Investor Warrants (together, the "Warrants"). This will result in the repayment of the deposit already paid for the Pre-Paid Warrants (less a contribution to transaction costs) to the cancelling holders in accordance with the terms of the Warrants. Following this cancellation, the Company has 41,666,667 Warrants outstanding.
Warrants extension details
Under the existing terms, Warrants are not capable of being exercised until certain conditions which are detailed in the 28 August 2025 announcement have been satisfied and would have expired if such conditions were not satisfied by 30 November 2025. The Company and the remaining warrant holders, including DeFi Development Corp, have agreed to extend the period within which these conditions may be satisfied to 1 June 2026. Accordingly, the Warrants will only become exercisable once the relevant conditions have been fulfilled on or before 1 June 2026.
If the conditions have not been satisfied by 1 June 2026, the Warrants will expire and be cancelled and the deposit for the Pre-Paid Warrants will be repaid to the relevant investors (less pre
‑
agreed contributions to transaction costs).
All other terms of the Warrants remain unchanged.
Pre-Paid Warrants and Investor Warrants have the meanings given to them in the announcement published on 28 August 2025.
For further information:
Cykel AI plc
Michael Chan

latest.title: Changes in Board Composition
latest.content:
3 Nov 2025 07:00
RNS Number : 7828F
Cykel AI PLC
03 November 2025
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended). Upon the publication of this announcement via a Regulatory Information Service, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain.
3 November 2025
Cykel AI PLC
("Cykel AI" or the "Company")
Changes in Board Composition
Cykel AI PLC (LSE:CYK) announces that Nicholas Lyth has resigned as Interim Chairman of the Company, with immediate effect.
The Board would like to take this opportunity to thank Mr. Lyth for his contributions to the Company and wish him well for the future.
Michael Chan will assume the role of Chairman on an interim basis pending the appointment of a Non-executive Chairman in due course.
For further information:
Cykel AI plc
Michael Chan

company_30s.what_they_do: Cykel AI builds AI‑powered sales agents and go‑to‑market solutions. Their flagship product, Eve, is a virtual sales assistant that can handle customer queries, qualify leads and close deals through chat and voice. They also offer GTM AI, a platform that helps businesses design and run automated marketing funnels.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global sales‑automation market is growing fast, driven by the need for cheaper, scalable customer engagement. Cykel AI’s technology plugs directly into existing CRM systems, giving businesses an edge in lead conversion and customer retention. By using advanced natural‑language processing, their agents can handle a wide range of industries, from fintech to e‑commerce, positioning the company to tap into a multi‑billion‑dollar opportunity.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is still in the early‑stage revenue generation phase but has secured multiple funding rounds to support product development and market expansion. Recent earnings show strong month‑on‑month growth, and the company has begun partnering with larger players such as Transpharmation. Cash burn remains high, but the capital raised should give them a runway of 12‑18 months if they hit the next milestone.

rns_narrative: Funding and capital structure are the main focus: a £2.8 million raise in August and a £750 k placement in May, coupled with a change of name and a Bitcoin treasury reserve strategy in January. These moves signal a desire to shore up liquidity and create a more resilient balance sheet.

Product and growth momentum is highlighted by the launch of Eve and GTM AI, a 68 % month‑on‑month revenue jump in June, and a commercial agreement with Transpharmation. These announcements suggest that the company is moving from prototype to revenue‑generating product.
Governance and communication are being tightened: board changes, an AGM, and regular interim results aim to improve transparency and investor confidence.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders point to the AI sales niche and recent product launches as a sign that the stock could rebound once the market takes notice.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Many voices caution that the company still lacks solid revenue and is burning cash. They worry that without a clear exit or sustained earnings, the share price could stay trapped.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Initially skeptical chatter has begun to shift toward cautious excitement as new product releases surface and funding rounds close.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is dead, so any timely news or earnings surprise could trigger a sharp reaction.

near_term_catalysts: The next quarterly earnings report will reveal whether the 68 % growth trend continues and if the company is moving toward profitability.
A new partnership announcement or a successful pilot with a large client could validate the product’s market fit and spark buying.
If the stock’s volume spikes at the 20‑day moving‑average support level, it would signal institutional interest and a potential mean‑reversion.
expect_next.watch_for: Keep an eye on earnings surprises, volume surges, and any sign of institutional buying. A jump in the 20‑day moving‑average or a breakout above the 50‑day moving‑average would confirm the thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is still in the early recovery phase of a deep crash. It needs a clear catalyst—ideally a positive earnings release or a high‑profile partnership—to validate that the market has recognized the value proposition.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The company sits deep in a crash, yet it has a credible AI product pipeline and recent funding that should support a turnaround. If the stock recovers to the midpoint of its all‑time high, the upside is roughly double the current level, while the downside risk is limited to the depth of the crash.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risks are the lack of sustained revenue and a heavy cash burn that could force a sale or liquidation if earnings don’t improve. A failure to secure new clients or a negative earnings report could push the price back to its trough.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is about 2 to 1 relative to the current level, with a downside limited to the depth of the crash. The asymmetry makes this a high‑reward, high‑risk play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price breaks above the 20‑day moving‑average after a period of low volume, placing a stop 5 % below that level. Allocate 10–15 % of the portfolio to this position and scale in if the trend continues.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the next earnings release or a partnership announcement. Watch for a volume spike and a breakout from the 20‑day support zone; that would be a clear entry trigger.
playbook.if_passing: None identified. No imminent delisting or bankruptcy filings have surfaced.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep‑crash AI sales firm with fresh funding and a promising product pipeline is primed for a mean‑reversion event; if it rebounds to the midpoint of its peak, the upside far outweighs the downside.
